description: Blog topic bank for openexperts.tech. Scheduled agent picks the next unused topic.

topics.title: What Happens When Your IT Guy Takes a Vacation?
topics.keywords: IT single point of failure, IT coverage, small business IT backup plan
topics.angle: The risk of depending on one person. What happens when they're sick, quit, or retire. How to build redundancy.

topics.title: Why Your Business Needs More Than Antivirus in 2026
topics.keywords: small business cybersecurity, antivirus not enough, endpoint protection, layered security
topics.angle: Antivirus alone hasn't been enough for years. Cover what modern threats look like and what a real security stack includes.

topics.title: The Real Cost of Free IT Advice From Your Nephew
topics.keywords: cheap IT support, amateur IT help, small business IT mistakes
topics.angle: Funny but real. The hidden costs of having unqualified people manage business-critical systems. Not mean-spirited, just honest.

topics.title: What to Do After a Data Breach: A Small Business Playbook
topics.keywords: data breach response, small business data breach, incident response plan
topics.angle: Step-by-step what to do in the first 24 hours, first week, and first month. Legal obligations, notification, remediation.

topics.title: Cloud vs On-Premise: Which Actually Makes Sense for Your Business?
topics.keywords: cloud vs on premise, small business cloud migration, should I move to cloud
topics.angle: Honest comparison. Cloud isn't always cheaper or better. On-prem isn't dead. Help them think through the decision.

topics.title: Why We Use Open Source (And Why You Should Care)
topics.keywords: open source for business, open source vs proprietary, free business software
topics.angle: Demystify open source. It's not sketchy free software. It powers the internet. Explain why it matters for their bottom line.

topics.title: Your Firewall Is Probably a Consumer Router. Here's Why That's a Problem.
topics.keywords: business firewall, consumer router vs firewall, pfSense small business, network security
topics.angle: Most small businesses are running a $60 router as their only protection. Explain what a real firewall does and why it matters.

topics.title: What Every Business Owner Should Know About Cyber Insurance
topics.keywords: cyber insurance small business, cyber liability insurance, do I need cyber insurance
topics.angle: What it covers, what it doesn't, why premiums are rising, and how better security lowers your rates.

topics.title: How to Survive an IT Provider Transition Without Losing Your Mind
topics.keywords: switching IT providers, changing IT companies, IT provider transition
topics.angle: Practical guide to making the switch. What to get from your current provider, what to expect, red flags during handoff.

topics.title: The Microsoft 365 Settings Most Businesses Get Wrong
topics.keywords: Microsoft 365 security settings, M365 configuration, Office 365 best practices
topics.angle: MFA not enabled, sharing defaults too open, no audit logging, retention policies misconfigured. Practical fixes.

topics.title: Why Your Business Needs a Password Manager Yesterday
topics.keywords: business password manager, password security, credential management small business
topics.angle: Sticky notes and spreadsheets aren't cutting it. What a password manager does, why it matters, how to roll one out.

topics.title: We Phished Our Own Clients. Here's What We Learned.
topics.keywords: phishing test, phishing simulation, employee security training, social engineering
topics.angle: Results from real phishing simulations. What people click, why, and how training dramatically reduces the risk.

topics.title: Does Your Business Have a Disaster Recovery Plan? (No, a Backup Isn't Enough)
topics.keywords: disaster recovery plan small business, business continuity, DR planning
topics.angle: Backups are one piece. What about RTO/RPO? Who does what? Communication plan? Walk through building a real DR plan.

topics.title: How Vendor Lock-In Is Quietly Costing Your Business Thousands
topics.keywords: vendor lock-in, IT vendor lock-in, proprietary software costs
topics.angle: When your IT provider or software vendor owns your data, passwords, or infrastructure. How to recognize it and break free.

topics.title: Your Remote Workers Are a Security Risk. Here's How to Fix That.
topics.keywords: remote work security, VPN for business, work from home IT security
topics.angle: Home networks, personal devices, public Wi-Fi. Real risks and practical solutions that don't make remote work miserable.

topics.title: The 5 Most Expensive IT Mistakes Small Businesses Make
topics.keywords: IT mistakes small business, common IT problems, small business technology mistakes
topics.angle: No backups tested, no security budget, buying consumer gear, no documentation, reactive-only IT. Real costs of each.

topics.title: Multi-Factor Authentication: The Single Best Thing You Can Do for Security Today
topics.keywords: MFA for business, two factor authentication, multi-factor authentication small business
topics.angle: What MFA is in plain English, why it stops 99% of account compromises, how to roll it out without driving your team crazy. Cover M365 MFA specifically.

topics.title: What's Hiding in Your Server Closet? (A Field Guide to IT Neglect)
topics.keywords: server room maintenance, small business server, IT infrastructure neglect
topics.angle: Funny but real tour of common server closet nightmares — daisy-chained power strips, consumer switches, no UPS, dust-choked vents. What each one risks and how to fix it.

topics.title: SharePoint vs a File Server: Which One Does Your Business Actually Need?
topics.keywords: SharePoint vs file server, OneDrive for business, small business file sharing
topics.angle: Honest comparison. SharePoint/OneDrive is great for some things, terrible for others. File servers aren't dead. Help them choose based on how their team actually works.

topics.title: A $200 UPS Could Save Your Business $50,000. Here's Why.
topics.keywords: UPS for business, uninterruptible power supply, power protection server, surge protection business
topics.angle: What happens when power blips — corrupted databases, dead drives, lost work. What a UPS does, how to size one, and why every business with a server needs one.

topics.title: Your Security Cameras Are on the Same Network as Your Business Data. That's a Problem.
topics.keywords: security camera network segmentation, IoT security business, network security cameras
topics.angle: IoT devices like cameras are notoriously insecure. If they're on the same network as your files and POS, they're a backdoor. Explain VLANs and segmentation in simple terms.

topics.title: Business Email Compromise: The $2.7 Billion Scam You've Never Heard Of
topics.keywords: business email compromise, BEC scam, CEO fraud, email fraud small business
topics.angle: Not phishing — targeted impersonation where attackers pretend to be the CEO or a vendor and trick employees into wiring money. Real examples, how to spot it, how to prevent it.

topics.title: If Your IT Person Got Hit by a Bus Tomorrow, Could You Recover?
topics.keywords: IT documentation, business continuity, IT knowledge transfer, bus factor
topics.angle: The bus factor problem. If everything lives in one person's head, you're one resignation away from chaos. What should be documented, how, and where.

topics.title: Windows 10 End of Life: What Your Business Needs to Do Before the Deadline
topics.keywords: Windows 10 end of life, Windows 10 EOL business, upgrade to Windows 11, Windows 10 support ending
topics.angle: October 2025 deadline passed but many businesses are still running it. Security risks of unpatched systems. Options: upgrade, replace hardware, or extended security updates (paid). Practical guidance.

topics.title: Managed IT vs Break-Fix: Which Model Actually Saves You Money?
topics.keywords: managed IT vs break fix, MSP vs break fix, managed services small business
topics.angle: Real cost comparison. Break-fix seems cheaper until something big breaks. Managed seems expensive until you realize what's included. Help them calculate the real math for their situation.

topics.title: Your Company's Passwords Are Probably Already on the Dark Web
topics.keywords: dark web monitoring business, compromised credentials, data breach check, password leak
topics.angle: What the dark web is (demystified), how credentials end up there, why it matters even for small businesses, and what you can do about it. Not fear-mongering — practical.

topics.title: AI for Small Business: What's Actually Useful vs What's Just Hype
topics.keywords: AI for small business, artificial intelligence small business, ChatGPT for business
topics.angle: Cut through the noise. What AI tools are genuinely useful right now (email drafting, data analysis, customer service). What's overhyped. Security and privacy concerns with business data in AI tools.

topics.title: How to Onboard and Offboard Employees Without Creating Security Gaps
topics.keywords: employee onboarding IT, employee offboarding security, IT checklist new hire, terminate employee access
topics.angle: New hire needs access to everything on day one. Departing employee shouldn't have access to anything after they leave. Most businesses botch both. Provide practical checklists for each.

topics.title: Why Your Business Wi-Fi Sucks (And How to Fix It for Under $500)
topics.keywords: business WiFi setup, slow office WiFi, access points vs router, small business wireless
topics.angle: Consumer routers daisy-chained. No guest network. Dead zones everywhere. Cover what proper business WiFi looks like — access points, controller-based management, guest isolation. Budget-friendly options.

topics.title: Data Compliance for Small Business: What You Probably Don't Know You're Required to Do
topics.keywords: small business data compliance, HIPAA small business, PCI compliance small business, data protection regulations
topics.angle: Many small businesses have compliance obligations they don't know about (PCI if you take cards, HIPAA if you touch health data, state privacy laws). Quick guide to figuring out what applies to you.

topics.title: When Was the Last Time You Tested Your Backups? (If You Can't Remember, Read This)
topics.keywords: backup testing, backup restore test, backup verification, are my backups working
topics.angle: Backups that have never been tested are Schrodinger's backups — they might work, they might not. Real horror stories of untested backups failing. How to build a testing routine.

topics.title: Your 7-Year-Old Server Is a Ticking Time Bomb. Here's the Math.
topics.keywords: server replacement cycle, hardware lifecycle, when to replace server, old server risk
topics.angle: The cost of running old hardware vs replacing it. Failure rates by age, energy costs, performance impact, warranty gaps. A simple framework for budgeting hardware replacement.

topics.title: Microsoft 365 Licensing: A Plain-English Guide to Not Overpaying
topics.keywords: Microsoft 365 licensing guide, M365 plans comparison, Office 365 which plan, Microsoft licensing small business
topics.angle: Business Basic vs Standard vs Premium. Which users need which plans. Common overspending mistakes. The features most small businesses actually use vs what they're paying for.

topics.title: Your Employees Will Give Away the Keys to the Kingdom If You Ask Nicely Enough
topics.keywords: social engineering attacks, social engineering prevention, employee security awareness
topics.angle: How social engineering works — phone pretexting, tailgating, impersonation. Real scenarios. Why even smart people fall for it. How training and process changes make it much harder.

topics.title: The Cheapest Security Upgrade You're Not Using: DNS Filtering
topics.keywords: DNS filtering business, DNS security, web filtering small business, block malicious websites
topics.angle: DNS filtering blocks malicious sites before they load. Takes 15 minutes to set up, costs almost nothing, stops a huge percentage of threats. One of the best bang-for-buck security measures.

topics.title: A 20-Point IT Audit You Can Do Yourself (Before Calling Anyone)
topics.keywords: IT audit checklist, small business IT assessment, IT health check DIY
topics.angle: Empowering, not sales-y. Give them 20 yes/no questions to assess their own IT health. Covers backups, security, documentation, hardware age, licensing, disaster recovery. Score yourself and know where you stand.

topics.title: Year-End IT Planning: How to Set Up 2027 for Success
topics.keywords: IT planning small business, technology budget planning, year end IT review, IT roadmap
topics.angle: Timely end-of-year piece. Review what happened this year, budget for next year, plan hardware refreshes, review contracts and renewals, assess security posture. A practical planning framework.
